hypothesis: Introducing a bulky aromatic residue at position 5 and a hydrophobic substitution at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity in the ECL2/TM5 interface, increasing selectivity margin by differentiating from off-target subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design protocol is generating a small number of candidates with strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient diversity to explore the full selectivity landscape. Relaxing the ddG threshold and increasing the candidate pool will improve the chances of identifying a more selective binder.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current ddG threshold is too stringent, filtering out potentially viable candidates with strong but slightly less optimal binding. Relaxing the threshold may increase the pass rate while still maintaining a focus on strong SSTR2 binding.

A small candidate pool limits the chances of generating a high-quality, selective binder. Increasing the number of candidates will improve diversity and the likelihood of hitting the selectivity target.
